reviews.review_description: This is a beautiful and fun game with an interesting story line. The only issue I had was the controller for the Go needed to be reset a lot because the position was changing so much. If you can’t click on things, remember to reset your position!

I got that enthusiastic and into the adventure, I started to walk around my living room ( not a good idea ) waving my arm about, and bosh..... I smashed the light ! 
Basically, it’s that good, you will end up getting off your sofa and start walking 😂

Would’ve given this 4 stars but.. took forever to complete because of a few bugs. The biggest one being not being able to move forward. Really sucks when you’re flying and all of a sudden you can’t fly forward, causing you to fall to your death.

People are saying that this is a good game and you will not regret getting it. I got this game listening to those people, and it is probably the first game I wasted my $10 on. The graphics are good, the soundtrack is good. The adventure and story is not. I've played through half of the game trying to get myself to like it, but it never worked. So far the game is very straightforward and boring. I really dont understand why people like this so much. I wish I could refund, but it's too late. I've already wasted more than 45 minutes on this game.
Wow. I am so blown away by this game. It was so good, it makes me wonder how amazing VR experiences will be in the future. Truly a masterful achievement in VR.

reviews.review_title: I can’t get the controls to work
reviews.review_description: The controls keep glitching out & it won’t allow me to move forward. Also the check points are making me start 20 mins back from where I already was. It’s completely dissuaded me from playing the game. I cannot progress because I can’t move my character. I bought a game I can’t play nor can I get a return.
Truly captivating. I played it until my Oculus Go ran out of power

I’ve had my Oculus Go for a little over six weeks now, and I’d had my sights set on this title from Day 1. The hype and accolades for this game had me worried that it would be a letdown. I just finished it today, and I can assure you it is as amazing as everyone says it is. All of the hyperbole thrown around is truly justified. Mesmerizing, spellbinding, awe-inspiring, it all applies. An earlier reviewer called it a cross between Land’s End and Daedalus, and that assessment is spot on. Every aspect of the game, from the graphics to the gameplay to the music, although not perfect (I’ll get to that), is still excellent and top-notch. It’s truly a journey that upon finishing, you feel like you have just experienced something special. It’s the same feeling I had when I was a teenager in the 90’s playing Out Of This World  on the PC. The opportunity to explore another world that leaves your jaw dropped every few minutes is unforgettable.

Of course, nothing is perfect. In response to the numerous complaints about the Go controller, I will say that, yes, there were moments of frustration while platforming. The core flying mechanic is borrowed heavily from Daedalus but that game has the mechanic locked in to almost perfection, and Eclipse wishes it were that precise. But still, these moments of frustration were fleeting and were not to the degree that I couldn’t figure it out or that I wanted to turn the game off. I never felt the immersion was ruined. Speaking of which, a swivel chair is a must for this game for the full effect. Much has also been said of the short length (approximately 3-4 hours). I feel that anything more would have started to feel tedious. Some of the best games of all time are such because they don’t try to do too much and don’t overstay their welcome.

In all, this is a truly GREAT virtual reality experience in every sense of the word. Do yourself a favor, and take a break from the adrenaline of most VR apps and quietly explore a world of beauty, mystery, spookiness, and grandeur. Games like this are why we bought the headset in the first place.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this amazing review.  I'll let the team know.  :)

reviews.review_description: 5 star deserved game that is not a Zombie shooting fest

I have been looking forward this to this being released on the Go ever since I played it on the Daydream. Definitely a must have VR experience. Wonderful visuals and fantastic soundtrack.  Very cool mystery storyline. The part where you are on the river is amazing.  Btw, if you are used to VR you can turn off the optical circle and it feels a lot more immersive. I definitely recommend that if VR doesn't make you feel sick.

A half an hour in, and I’m starting to used to the controls. I am a frequent player of vr and have developed for several headsets. 

This game does great with just a headset and controller. It emphasizes on the control possibilities with the OGo.

Dev, if you are reading this, I really wish there were more settings. The movement is too quick sometimes and it causes my to feel sick. (Standing or sitting).

Issues I had, sometimes the player moves differently then you expect when moving around corners. And the jetpack can be frustrating because its elevation is not consistent.

The graphics were awesome in the game. It is user friendly, the first part of the game teaches you how to use the controller and what to do with it. 
The rest of the game is kind of like a puzzle. You have to figure out how to get into the next room. It's not so complex that you feel like quitting but it's complex enough to keep interest. 
You do have to throw the artifact often so make sure you're not near something breakable when you do. I accidentally punched the wall one of the times haha. 
Very entertaining. 
The only con was that it did not save all of my progress when my oculas died so I had to redo 3 of the realities but they went quickly since I already knew what I was doing. 
Be safe and have fun!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review!  You can restart from the last checkpoint to start close to where the game last stopped.  Or select the Act menus to restart from one of the 5 acts.

reviews.review_description: It really feels like you are there. It is even a little creepy at times when the statues turn to look at you. This is probably my new favorite game up there with virtual virtual reality.

its fantastic! if youre a dinosaur like me, who likes to take her time, gaze at the scenery, walk around, jump around to explore, this is a game for you. the moment when the moon crashes into you is super freaky! worth the purchase!!!

reviews.review_title: Yes, very nice if a bit short
reviews.review_description: The movement is by track pad like Slightly Heros which is great.  Jetpacking is fun.  The story was mediocre and told in an excoriating slow way...realistically the story lasts about 2 minutes to tell.  But, the gameplay is fun and felt very immersive.  I would like to see more of this type of game.  There were a few parts I felt I needed to hold on to something like the big pipes near the end.  Very good.  Maybe tell the story better in your next one.
I highly recommend this game some great visuals along the way and just a great VR exp.

Just purchased this and so far blown away.   
I'm using the Oculus go and so far the only problem I'm having is gazing at my wristband to bring up the menu. It's a  real pain, the circle starts and stops  and takes 5,6,7 tries to complete. Can you please look into this.  Other then that very smooth gameplay

developer_response.body: Hi, You only need to look at your wrist if you want to change in options, but this can also be done from the main menu.  Tips for the wrist menu is to stay still while this is happening and then executing the button press.  Hope this helps!

reviews.review_description: It is a pretty immersive and beautiful VR world, and I love all the puzzle solving. I suspect it would be much better on Rift or other system that has a better interface; as is I found it very difficult to maneuver in the air with the little oculus go controller. It actually got so glitchy trying to fly around that I gave up on the game before I finished it. Maybe with a better controller.

I've had my go 2 days and just discovered the 360 swivel chair. This game is awesome so far. Just exploring the environment is jar dropping. The early game of fetch with the alien Rhinos was a great touch
I can’t get past the first few minutes as I can’t move forward while jumping. Too frustrated to do much more. Controls are not intuitive and very clunky
